#1 QLYS Mid Cap Qualys, Inc. · Technology HIGH ACT NOW Quality Compounder Stalwart Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Qualys is a cloud-native cybersecurity platform offering vulnerability management, threat detection, and compliance solutions delivered as SaaS to enterprises globally. Customers pay recurring subscription fees for continuous security posture visibility across their IT environments. Revenue is almost entirely subscription-based with near-zero churn among enterprise accounts.

Industry: Cloud Cybersecurity / Vulnerability Management  ·  vs: Tenable Holdings, Rapid7, CrowdStrike (CSPM module)

"Six specialists agree: the market is pricing a melting legacy vendor while the fundamentals show a 46% FCF margin compounder nobody wants to talk about"

Consensus has written QLYS off as a 'boring' VM vendor losing share to AI-native peers, which is why it trades at PE 15.4 despite 28% ROIC and 46% FCF margin — metrics that put it in the top 5% of all software companies by capital efficiency. Insiders are buying at 53% of 52-week highs. The market misses that QLYS's agent-based architecture is actually well-positioned for the AI security era: continuous exposure management is accelerating as a budget priority, not shrinking. At $3B market cap with 75% DCF margin of safety and net cash of 12% of market cap, this is a quality compounder priced like a distressed asset.

Price $82.14 PEG 1.6 P/E 15.4 ROIC 28.1% Catalyst Cybersecurity consolidation wave: QLYS at 15.4x PE and 46% FCF margin is a textbook acquisition target for a larger platform (CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, or PE) — any M&A announcement would be a 50-80% premium event; independently, Q2 2026 earnings showing accelerating platform adoption metrics would begin multiple re-rating. Watch Competitive displacement by Palo Alto Networks bundling VM capabilities free within its Cortex platform — if enterprise accounts begin dropping QLYS for bundled PANW, the growth rate thesis breaks.

#2 PTCT Mid Cap PTC Therapeutics, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Fast Grower Fast Grower ▼
🏢 About the Business

PTC Therapeutics is a rare-disease biopharmaceutical company focused on CNS disorders including Duchenne muscular dystrophy and other RNA-splicing-related diseases. Revenue is generated through product sales (Translarna in Europe) and milestone/royalty payments from partners including Roche. The company has pivoted to a royalty-augmented revenue model as its Huntington's disease asset (tominersen, via Roche) drives milestone income.

Industry: Rare Disease / CNS Specialty Pharma  ·  vs: Sarepta Therapeutics, Wave Life Sciences, Solid Biosciences

"Five specialists: the market is still modeling last year's -14% revenue decline while a 115% growth inflection and 37% ROIC are already in the financial statements"

PTCT's revenue went from -14% to +115% YoY — the single largest acceleration in this entire candidate universe — driven by tominersen milestones from Roche and commercial expansion of its rare disease portfolio. The market is anchored to the prior-year contraction narrative and pricing the stock at PE 8.7 despite 37% ROIC, 40% FCF margin, and 91% gross margins. Additionally, EU Translarna is fully operational, and a potential US re-submission pathway for Translarna represents unpriced call-option value that could double the US-addressable revenue overnight. Net cash at 24% of market cap provides the balance sheet backstop.

Price $72.22 PEG — P/E 8.7 ROIC 37.4% Catalyst Roche's tominersen Phase 3 readout for Huntington's disease expected in 2026 — positive data would trigger additional milestone payments and validate the platform value, likely causing a sharp re-rating from PE 8.7 toward peer multiples of 20-25x. Watch Tominersen Phase 3 failure in Huntington's disease would eliminate a major milestone stream and validate the bears' concern about over-reliance on a partner-funded pipeline; FDA continued non-approval of Translarna in the US remains a structural ceiling on domestic revenue.

#3 HRB Mid Cap H&R Block, Inc. · Consumer Cyclical HIGH ACT NOW IV Discount Slow Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

H&R Block provides tax preparation services to individuals and small businesses through a network of approximately 9,000 retail locations and digital platforms. Revenue comes primarily from assisted tax preparation fees (60%+ of revenue), software subscriptions, and small business financial services. The company has a loyal base of complexity-averse, lower-income filers who prefer human preparer assistance over self-directed tools.

Industry: Consumer Tax Services  ·  vs: Intuit TurboTax, Jackson Hewitt, IRS Direct File (free government tool)

"Five specialists: the AI-kills-tax-prep narrative has pushed a 30% ROIC franchise to 6.4x earnings — one of the cheapest high-quality franchises in the entire consumer sector"

The market has priced HRB as if IRS Direct File and TurboTax will permanently disrupt the assisted tax segment, but this narrative ignores a fundamental customer segmentation reality: HRB's core client is complexity-averse, not tech-averse — they have consistently chosen human preparers even as DIY options proliferated for two decades. With insiders buying aggressively, 30% ROIC, 13% FCF yield, 5.3% dividend, and 6% buyback yield, HRB delivers 11%+ annual cash return to shareholders while the market waits for disruption that hasn't materially arrived. At PE 6.4, the stock is priced for permanent structural decline — a narrative that 4% revenue consistency over 5 years explicitly contradicts.

Price $32.09 PEG 0.5 P/E 6.4 ROIC 30.1% Catalyst FY2026 tax season results (typically disclosed April-May) showing assisted segment revenue stability or growth will directly disprove the IRS Direct File disruption narrative with hard data, likely triggering a multiple re-rating toward 10-12x PE from current 6.4x. Watch A material expansion of IRS Direct File to include more complex return types (Schedule C, rental income) would structurally threaten HRB's core market — monitor Congressional appropriations for IRS technology budget each fiscal year.

#4 PLMR Mid Cap Palomar Holdings, Inc. · Financial Services HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Fast Grower Fast Grower Cyclical Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Palomar is a specialty insurance company focused on catastrophe-exposed lines — earthquake, hurricane, flood, and other perils that standard carriers have retreated from. It earns underwriting income from premiums net of reinsurance costs, with a capital-light model that transfers peak catastrophe risk to reinsurers while retaining attractive attritional profits. Distribution is through independent agents and partnerships.

Industry: Specialty Catastrophe Insurance  ·  vs: Hippo Holdings, Openly (Homepoint), standard carriers retreating (Allstate, State Farm)

"Four specialists: 58% revenue growth and 46% FCF margin in specialty insurance trading at PEG 0.7 — the market is confusing premium growth with unhedged loss exposure"

Standard carriers are exiting California, Florida, and other catastrophe-exposed states at an accelerating pace, creating a structural demand windfall for specialist underwriters like Palomar. The market prices PLMR as if it carries the same catastrophe risk as the departing carriers, missing that Palomar's reinsurance structure transfers peak-event losses while retaining the very profitable attritional book. Revenue is accelerating from 47% to 58% growth, FCF margin is 46%, and the stock sits 25% below its 52-week high. At PEG 0.7, the market is paying slow-grower multiples for a structural growth story with a decade-long tailwind.

Price $132.32 PEG 0.7 P/E 17.5 ROIC 8.8% Catalyst Q2 2026 earnings (July) showing continued >50% premium growth following 2025 California fire season would confirm the structural demand thesis; any announcement of a new state expansion or partnership with a major MGU would accelerate the re-rating. Watch A single catastrophic loss event (major California earthquake or Gulf hurricane) in a year where Palomar has grown its retained book faster than its reinsurance protection could cause a material earnings miss — the reinsurance structure is the thesis, and any gaps in that structure are existential.

#5 HALO Mid Cap Halozyme Therapeutics, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Fast Grower Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Halozyme owns the ENHANZE drug delivery technology platform, which enables subcutaneous delivery of biologics that would otherwise require intravenous infusion. It licenses this technology to major pharmaceutical partners (Roche, Janssen, Pfizer, Argenx, and others) who pay milestones and royalties as their ENHANZE-enabled drugs reach commercial scale. Halozyme does not develop or sell drugs itself — it is a pure royalty platform.

Industry: Pharmaceutical Drug Delivery / Royalty Platform  ·  vs: Antares Pharma (acquired), Enable Injections, Credence Medical — no direct platform equivalent at scale

"Revenue accelerating from 22% to 38% as multiple blockbuster partner drugs simultaneously hit commercial inflection — the royalty flywheel is compounding faster than consensus models"

Halozyme is structurally misunderstood as a 'biotech' when it is actually a royalty collection machine with 82% gross margins and 58% operating margins. The market models each ENHANZE partnership drug individually and misses the portfolio inflection: as Argenx's efgartigimod, Roche's Ocrevus Zunovo, and Pfizer's Darzalex SC simultaneously enter growth phases, the royalty streams compound non-linearly. Forward PE collapses from 25.3x trailing to 7.9x on a 12-month forward basis — this is one of the most dramatic forward earnings normalization stories in the entire candidate universe. FCF margin of 46% and active buybacks (6% annually) confirm the capital returns story is accelerating.

Price $70.05 PEG 0.7 P/E 25.3 ROIC 23.6% Catalyst H1 2026 royalty revenue disclosures showing Darzalex SC and Ocrevus Zunovo reaching combined royalty run-rates implying forward PE normalization toward 10-12x would trigger analyst estimate revisions; additionally, any new ENHANZE partnership announcement with a top-10 pharma company is an immediate re-rating event. Watch The EG5y estimate of only +2% suggests consensus is deeply uncertain about long-term royalty duration — if partner drugs face competitive displacement or IRA price negotiation reduces sales, the royalty base could plateau earlier than expected.

#6 ODD Small Cap ODDITY Tech Ltd. · Consumer Cyclical HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Fast Grower Fast Grower ▼
🏢 About the Business

ODDITY is a consumer technology company that sells beauty and wellness products (IL MAKIAGE cosmetics, Spoiled Child skincare) direct-to-consumer via AI-powered personalization tools. Customers complete AI-driven skin/hair quizzes that generate highly personalized product recommendations and subscription reorders. Revenue is generated through D2C product sales and recurring subscription purchases, with the AI platform creating progressively better recommendations as the dataset grows.

Industry: AI-Powered D2C Beauty / Consumer Tech  ·  vs: Estee Lauder (traditional), L'Oreal (traditional), Function of Beauty (niche D2C)

"Four specialists: the market assigns a hardware-company multiple to a 73% gross margin AI data platform because it sells mascara — one of the most obvious category mislabelings in small-cap"

ODDITY is systematically misclassified as a beauty company when its actual moat is a proprietary AI data platform: 73% gross margins and 25% revenue growth at PE 7.7 make no sense for a cosmetics retailer — they make complete sense for a software-driven subscription engine. Every transaction trains the model, improving recommendations, increasing repurchase rates, and widening the personalization gap vs. traditional beauty brands. The market misses the compounding data moat: the longer ODDITY operates, the harder its AI becomes to replicate with a blank dataset. Foreign-listed, undercovered by US sell-side, and misclassified in screeners — the Wall Street Blindspot thesis is validated by objective facts.

Price $15.06 PEG 0.8 P/E 7.7 ROIC — Catalyst Launch of ODDITY's third brand (pipeline disclosed in investor communications for 2026) would demonstrate platform scalability beyond two brands and trigger a re-rating from 'two-brand company' to 'brand-building platform' — this is the key narrative unlock. Watch Beauty is discretionary — any consumer spending deterioration could slow subscriber acquisition and increase churn among existing cohorts; additionally, ODDITY's foreign listing (Israel-based, US-listed) creates jurisdiction-specific risks that US institutional mandates may restrict.

#7 VITL Small Cap Vital Farms, Inc. · Consumer Defensive HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Fast Grower Fast Grower ▼
🏢 About the Business

Vital Farms produces and sells pasture-raised eggs and butter under its branded label, sourced from a network of family farms following rigorous animal welfare and pasture standards. Products are sold through 24,000+ retail locations nationwide at a significant premium to conventional eggs. Revenue is driven by branded product volume growth and maintained premium pricing across grocery, natural food, and club channels.

Industry: Premium Branded Food / Ethical Agriculture  ·  vs: Cal-Maine Foods (conventional scale), Pete & Gerry's (organic), Vital Farms has no direct pasture-raised peer at national scale

"Four specialists: the market assigns Cal-Maine commodity multiples to a 25% growth branded platform — a profound category error that PEG 0.4 makes undeniable"

Vital Farms is growing revenue 25-28% annually with 100% revenue consistency, PE 8.5, and PEG 0.4 — metrics that would generate enormous institutional excitement in tech, but are invisible when attached to 'egg company.' The market's category error is treating VITL as a commodity agricultural producer when it is a branded consumer platform with demonstrated pricing power: during the 2022-2024 conventional egg price spike, VITL maintained and expanded its premium without volume loss — definitive proof that its consumer franchise is structural, not cyclical. The $100B+ ethical food TAM and 6% current penetration in pasture-raised specifically suggest a long compounding runway.

Price $12.62 PEG 0.4 P/E 8.5 ROIC — Catalyst Q1 2026 earnings (May) showing continued same-store velocity gains at major grocery chains and new distribution wins would confirm the national footprint expansion thesis; any club channel (Costco/Sam's) volume disclosure would be a significant re-rating catalyst given the implied volume at that channel. Watch A major avian flu outbreak affecting Vital Farms' farm-partner network could simultaneously disrupt supply and damage brand perception — the geographically distributed but small-farm-dependent supply chain is structurally more vulnerable to disease events than industrial producers.

#8 ANIP Small Cap ANI Pharmaceuticals, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH WITHIN MONTHS Fast Grower Fast Grower ▼
🏢 About the Business

ANI Pharmaceuticals is a specialty pharmaceutical company that develops, manufactures, and commercializes branded and generic drug products, with a growing rare-disease franchise anchored by Cortrophin Gel (ACTH, used in inflammatory and autoimmune conditions). Revenue comes from branded rare-disease product sales, generic drug manufacturing, and contract manufacturing. The company is actively building out Cortrophin's indications beyond its initial neurology approvals.

Industry: Specialty / Rare Disease Pharma  ·  vs: Mallinckrodt (Acthar Gel, post-bankruptcy), Lundbeck, Jazz Pharmaceuticals

"Four specialists: Wall Street prices ANI as a generic drug rollup while a rare-disease platform with 44% revenue growth and blockbuster characteristics is being systematically ignored"

ANI is executing one of the more compelling specialty pharma transformations visible in small-cap: it acquired Cortrophin Gel at a distressed valuation and is now growing it aggressively into multiple underpenetrated indications while the generic business provides cash flow. Revenue growth re-accelerated to +44% YoY, gross margins are 69%, PEG is 0.5, and 100% revenue consistency confirms this is not a one-quarter phenomenon. The market prices it as a commodity generic manufacturer because sell-side coverage has not yet transitioned to covering it as a rare-disease franchise — the coverage gap is the opportunity.

Price $81.72 PEG 0.5 P/E 20.4 ROIC — Catalyst Cortrophin's penetration data in rheumatology and nephrology to be disclosed in H1 2026 earnings; any new indication approval or label expansion filing would be a binary positive re-rating event that the market has not priced into PEG 0.5. #9 NICE Mid Cap NICE Ltd. · Technology MEDIUM WITHIN MONTHS IV Discount Stalwart Fast Grower Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

NICE provides cloud-based customer experience software (CXone platform — the leading cloud contact center solution), workforce management tools, and financial crime analytics (Actimize). Revenue is predominantly recurring SaaS subscription from enterprise contact centers globally, with a secondary revenue stream from on-premise software maintenance and professional services. The company serves 25,000+ enterprise customers across financial services, healthcare, and retail.

Industry: Cloud Contact Center / Customer Experience Software  ·  vs: Genesys (private), Verint Systems, Five9, Salesforce Service Cloud

"Four specialists: the market fears AI disrupts NICE's contact center while NICE is actually the AI layer being deployed inside those contact centers — a classic narrative-vs-numbers gap"

NICE is priced at PE 10.4 with PEG 0.5 on 22% five-year EPS growth estimates — the market is pricing it as a legacy software vendor facing obsolescence, but the reality is that CXone Mpower (its AI-native platform) is positioned to benefit from, not be disrupted by, AI agent adoption in contact centers. Every enterprise deploying AI for customer service needs an orchestration, compliance, and quality management layer — NICE provides exactly that. The Actimize financial crime analytics business is a separately valuable asset trading at zero premium inside the current valuation. At 83% margin of safety and 100% revenue consistency, this is among the most compelling risk/reward setups in enterprise software.

Price $105.39 PEG 0.5 P/E 10.4 ROIC 13.3% Catalyst A potential Actimize separation or strategic review (explicitly flagged by Burry specialist) would unlock sum-of-parts value and force the market to price CXone and Actimize independently — each would likely receive a premium vs. the current blended discount multiple. Duolingo's AI models are trained on billions of user interaction datapoints, improving learning efficacy continuously.

"Three specialists: the market is confusing 'tech stock selloff' with 'business deterioration' — Duolingo at 81% below 52-week high is growing 39% with 36% FCF margins"

#11 EXEL Large Cap Exelixis, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH WITHIN MONTHS IV Discount Stalwart Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Exelixis is a specialty oncology pharmaceutical company whose primary commercial asset is Cabometyx (cabozantinib), approved across multiple indications in renal cell carcinoma, hepatocellular carcinoma, and thyroid cancer. Revenue is generated through US net product revenue from Cabometyx sales and royalties from partner licenses (including BMS-Opdivo combination). The company maintains a net-cash balance sheet funded by Cabometyx's high-margin cash generation and is actively investing in a pipeline of next-generation oncology compounds.

Industry: Oncology Specialty Pharma  ·  vs: Pfizer (Sutent, Inlyta), BMS (Nivolumab combinations), Merck (Keytruda combinations)

"ROIC 30%, gross margin 96%, net cash — the market prices Exelixis as a one-drug patent cliff while insiders loading near highs see a multi-indication platform with 82% margin of safety"

EXEL has 96% gross margins (highest in the specialty pharma peer group), 30% ROIC, 37% FCF margin, and $0 net debt — characteristics that would command a 25-30x multiple in any other sector. Instead it trades at PE 15.4 with an 82% DCF margin of safety because the market has decided Cabometyx is a one-drug story facing patent risk. Insiders are buying near 52-week highs, which directly contradicts this fear narrative — they see pipeline diversification (XL092 in multiple tumor types, zanzalintinib Phase 3) transforming Exelixis from a single-drug company into a multi-asset oncology platform with royalties providing perpetual compounding. Active buybacks at 6% annually add to the per-share compounding.

Price $44.10 PEG 1.1 P/E 15.4 ROIC 29.7% Catalyst Phase 3 data for zanzalintinib (XL092) in clear cell RCC expected in 2026 — positive results would validate the next-generation cabozantinib platform and address the patent cliff narrative directly by demonstrating a commercial successor to Cabometyx. Watch Cabometyx generic entry risk — if patent litigation produces an earlier-than-expected generic launch date, the single-product revenue concentration becomes an acute problem; monitor patent litigation calendar for RCC patents specifically.

#12 BAH Mid Cap Booz Allen Hamilton Holding Corporation · Industrials HIGH WITHIN WEEKS IV Discount Stalwart Fast Grower Cyclical Turnaround ▼
🏢 About the Business

Booz Allen Hamilton provides management consulting, analytics, digital transformation, and cybersecurity services exclusively to US federal government agencies, with the highest concentration in defense, intelligence, and national security clients. Revenue is generated through long-term government contracts (cost-plus and fixed-price) for technology, analytics, and advisory services. The company's classified contract portfolio is its most durable revenue source.

Industry: Government IT / National Security Consulting  ·  vs: SAIC, Leidos, Accenture Federal Services

"Three specialists: the market is pricing DOGE-apocalypse indiscriminately across all government contractors while BAH's classified national security backlog is materially insulated from civilian budget cuts"

The DOGE-driven government spending narrative has compressed BAH to 62% of its 52-week high with an 85% DCF margin of safety — but this ignores a critical distinction: BAH's revenue is 70%+ concentrated in defense, intelligence, and national security — agencies that historically maintain or increase budgets even during civilian agency cuts. Insiders know this, which is why they are buying. At PE 12.1 with ROIC 18%, 100% revenue consistency, and FCF yield of 9%, BAH meets every quality and valuation gate while the market conflates it with civilian IT contractors that are genuinely at risk.

Price $81.11 PEG 1.2 P/E 12.1 ROIC 18.1% Catalyst Q1/Q2 FY2027 earnings (scheduled May-August 2026) showing contract backlog stability or growth in the classified segment would definitively disprove the DOGE-disruption narrative for BAH specifically and trigger analyst estimate revisions upward from current depressed levels. Watch A genuine broad-based defense sequestration — not just civilian agency cuts but actual DoD and intelligence community spending cuts — would directly impair BAH's revenue; this is a low-probability but not-zero risk if political dynamics shift sharply in FY2027 budget negotiations.
Agents: 👁️ Insider Track 🎲 Pabrai Asym. 🔄 Marks 2nd-Level

⚠ Key Risks

  • Rate re-acceleration risk: if CPI re-accelerates above 4% and forces the Fed to hike, growth multiples compress further — most damaging to DUOL and RDDT where valuation still relies on forward earnings normalization
  • China geopolitical escalation: ATAT carries full China-listed risk; any Taiwan-related sanctions event or ADR delisting action makes the net-cash asset story irrelevant as foreign investors lose access — size accordingly
  • Government spending cuts (DOGE effect): BAH is explicitly exposed to discretionary IT budget cuts; if classified-mission-critical contracts face sequestration in Q2-Q3 2026, the 85% MoS thesis compresses — watch contract backlog disclosures quarterly

Industry: Consumer Tax Services / Financial Services · vs: Intuit TurboTax, Jackson Hewitt, Liberty Tax

"IV Discount: ROIC 30%, FCF yield 13%, PE 6.4x, PEG 0.5, MoS 76% — deeply mispriced relative to durable earnings power; catalyst is accelerating AI-assisted DIY tax product rollout (Block's DIY+ platform) and ongoing aggressive capital return (6% buyback run-rate + 5.3% dividend yield), with $4.1B market cap vs. ~$5.5B+ intrinsic value implied by 10x normalized FCF on $540M+ annual FCF generation."

Market penalizes HRB for secular DIY-shift fears while missing that AI augments rather than disrupts their assisted-tax moat, and the 30% ROIC + 13% FCF yield means the stock is a 6x PE self-liquidating compounder.

LEQVIO (partnered with Novartis) addresses a $20B+ cardiovascular prevention TAM and is still in early commercial ramp. Dilution check: minimal equity issuance at this stage. RNAi's network effect is scientific: each clinical dataset makes the platform more validated, attracting more partnership capital and reducing regulatory risk for follow-on programs."

The market is still valuing Alnylam on trailing losses; the forward earnings picture shows a genomics platform company crossing into sustained profitability with a $20B+ cardiovascular TAM barely penetrated.

Revenue +69% YoY with 100% consistency, gross margin 91%, FCF margin 31%, net cash, zero dilution, forward PE 11.2x versus trailing 57x. AltZ of 60 signals near-zero financial distress. The network effect is structural: Reddit's value comes from communities that become more valuable with more participants, creating a flywheel that Google, Meta, and X cannot simply replicate."

Wall Street sees a social media advertiser; the real business is the world's most valuable repository of authentic human knowledge, uniquely positioned as essential AI training infrastructure with secular pricing power.

Nick Sleep's framework that identified Amazon and Costco early — companies that share scale benefits with customers rather than capturing them, creating flywheels.
MELI Large Cap MercadoLibre, Inc. · Consumer Cyclical HIGH Fast Grower Cyclical ▼
39% revenue growth with 100% revenue consistency and FCFMargin=35% — compounding at scale
🏢 About
MercadoLibre operates Latin America's dominant e-commerce marketplace and fintech ecosystem (Mercado Pago), generating revenue from transaction fees, advertising, credit, and logistics services. It serves tens of millions of buyers, sellers, and unbanked consumers across Brazil, Mexico, Argentina, and 15+ other countries. The company reinvests aggressively into fulfillment infrastructure, lowering delivery costs and times for merchants.
Industry: E-Commerce / Fintech (Latin America) · vs: Amazon, Shopee, Rappi

"SES flywheel: more GMV → cheaper fulfillment per unit → lower fees for merchants → more sellers → more buyers → more GMV. Gross margin held intentionally at 45% while scaling from $3B to $20B+ revenue — evidence of sharing economics rather than extracting them. Customer obsession proof: Mercado Envios logistics network was built at massive capex loss for years specifically to make shipping free/cheap for small merchants who couldn't afford it alone. ROIC=14%, RG=+39% consistent, FCFMargin=35%, RevConsist=100%, PEG=1.2 vs 39% earnings growth."

Market prices MELI as a maturing e-commerce play, missing that Mercado Pago's credit/payments flywheel is still in its first innings of monetizing 60M+ active fintech users across underbanked LatAm.

🏢 About
Remitly is a digital remittance platform enabling immigrants to send money internationally to family in 170+ destination countries at lower cost than traditional wire transfer incumbents. It earns revenue from transaction fees and exchange rate spreads. Its customers are primarily working-class immigrants sending small, recurring transfers home.
Industry: Digital Remittance / Cross-Border Payments · vs: Western Union, MoneyGram, Wise

"SES flywheel: more transfer volume → lower corridor costs per transaction → lower fees passed to senders → more senders → more volume. Gross margin at 59% while scaling +29-34% annually — intentionally not expanding margin as scale grows, instead reducing fees to undercut Western Union's 5-7% take rate with sub-2% pricing on key corridors. Customer obsession proof: Remitly publicly commits to a 'Price Promise' guaranteeing locked exchange rates — absorbing FX volatility risk itself rather than passing it to vulnerable immigrant customers. MoS=82%, NetCash=+14%, AltZ=5.45, PEG=1.5."

Market misunderstands Remitly as a thin-margin commodity, missing that its trust-flywheel in immigrant communities (word-of-mouth referrals within diaspora networks) creates near-zero customer acquisition cost at scale.

Market sees monday.com as a commoditized project tool; the moat is actually the 200+ app marketplace and cross-functional workflow lock-in that makes replacement cost prohibitive once three departments depend on it.

Customers are global primary insurance carriers seeking peak catastrophe risk transfer.
Industry: Catastrophe Reinsurance / Specialty Reinsurance · vs: Everest Re, Munich Re, Swiss Re

"PE=5.2 and PEG=0.6 with ROE=24% and FCF yield=27% — dramatically cheaper than specialty insurance peers at 12-15x earnings — while the stock sits at 100% of its 52-week high suggesting the discount is structural ignorance, not distress. The specific catalyst is the hard reinsurance pricing cycle now in its third year post-Ian/Turkey earthquakes: RNR's 2025-2026 policy renewals are locked in at historically elevated Cat XL rates, and Q2 2026 earnings will reveal the full margin expansion from these renewals, forcing a re-rate from trough-cycle multiples to mid-cycle 10-12x PE."

The market is still pricing RNR on catastrophe-year loss fears while locking in 3+ years of hard-market pricing creates a structurally higher earnings baseline that will become undeniable at the June 2026 renewal disclosure.

Customers include Fortune 500 companies, government entities, and managed security service providers.
Industry: Cloud Security / Vulnerability Management · vs: Tenable, Rapid7, CrowdStrike

"InsiderTrack: ★×4 multi-strategy signal with insider buying flagged — executives purchasing at 53% of 52-week high into a cybersecurity selloff. Confirmation: FCFMargin=46%, GrMgn=83%, ROIC=28%, net cash balance sheet (NDE=-0.6), and 100% revenue consistency form a fortress-quality business insiders are backing with personal capital."

The market is discounting Qualys as a legacy VM vendor losing to broader platforms, but insiders buying at 53% of highs see the 46% FCF margin and net-cash balance sheet as deeply undervalued relative to durable 10% organic growth.

Price $82 P/E 15.4 PEG 1.6 ROIC 28.1% MktCap $3.0B
EXEL Large Cap Exelixis, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH Stalwart Turnaround ▼
GrMgn=96% with OprMgn=38% and ROIC=30% — net cash funded
🏢 About
Exelixis is an oncology-focused biopharmaceutical company that discovers, develops, and commercializes cancer therapeutics. Its primary revenue driver is cabozantinib (Cabometyx), approved across multiple tumor types including renal cell carcinoma and hepatocellular carcinoma. Revenue comes from U.S. product sales and international royalties/collaborations.
Industry: Specialty Oncology Pharma · vs: Bristol-Myers Squibb, Pfizer Oncology, Ipsen

"InsiderTrack: Insider buying cluster flagged with executives purchasing at 89% of 52-week high — strong conviction signal in a name near highs. Confirmation: ROIC=30%, GrMgn=96%, OprMgn=38%, RevConsist=100%, net cash (NDE=-0.3), and 6% net buyback yield confirm a capital-light compounder with a deep pipeline runway insiders are aggressively backing."

The market treats Exelixis as a one-drug story with patent expiry risk, but insiders loading near highs see pipeline diversification and 96% gross margins as a platform business being systematically undervalued.

Price $44 P/E 15.4 PEG 1.1 ROIC 29.7% MktCap $11.4B
🔍 Wall St Blindspot
Finds quality companies that fall below institutional radar — too small for major bank coverage, creating persistent mispricings patient investors can exploit.
ODD Small Cap Oddity Tech Ltd. · Technology HIGH Fast Grower ▼
PE=7.7 on 25% revenue growth (PEG=0.8) with 73% gross margin
🏢 About
Oddity Tech is an Israel-based direct-to-consumer beauty and wellness platform operating brands IL MAKIAGE and Spokin. It uses proprietary AI/data technology to match consumers with beauty products online, bypassing traditional retail. Revenue comes from direct product sales with a high repeat-purchase subscription-like model.
Industry: AI-Powered D2C Beauty Technology · vs: e.l.f. Beauty, Coty, Revlon

"Wall Street Blindspot: $0.9B Mcap with ~1 sell-side analyst (non-US-listed Israeli company on Nasdaq, confuses screeners as 'Technology' not beauty), 73% gross margin + 15% operating margin + PE of 7.7 on 25% revenue growth = PEG of 0.8 in a sector averaging 2.1; net cash balance sheet (DE=0.1) with CR=5.2 means zero financial risk."

The market prices this at a hardware-company multiple despite software-level margins and compounding D2C revenue growth because it's foreign-listed and misclassified as generic tech.

Price $15 P/E 7.7 PEG 0.8 MktCap $0.9B
DCBO Small Cap Docebo Inc. · Technology HIGH Fast Grower ▼
PEG=0.2 with 80% gross margin and 100% revenue consistency
🏢 About
Docebo is a Canadian-headquartered cloud-based Learning Management System (LMS) provider selling SaaS subscriptions to mid-market and enterprise corporate clients globally. Customers use the platform to train employees, partners, and customers. Revenue is almost entirely recurring subscription-based.
Industry: Enterprise SaaS / Corporate Learning Management · vs: Cornerstone OnDemand, SAP SuccessFactors, Absorb LMS

"Wall Street Blindspot: $0.5B Mcap Canadian-listed company (TSX cross-listed) with effectively 0 dedicated US sell-side analysts; 80% gross margin, 100% revenue consistency, active buyback (-5%), PEG=0.2 on 14% growth is deeply mispriced vs. SaaS sector peers trading at PEG 3-5x; ADV of $525K is flagged thin but Nasdaq US listing provides sufficient liquidity for small-cap mandates."

A high-quality SaaS compounder priced at a distressed multiple purely because Canadian small-caps fall into a jurisdictional coverage gap for US sell-side.

Price $17 P/E 12.3 PEG 0.2 MktCap $0.5B
ANIP Small Cap ANI Pharmaceuticals · Healthcare HIGH Fast Grower ▼
44% revenue growth with 69% gross margin at PEG=0.5
🏢 About
ANI Pharmaceuticals is a specialty pharmaceutical company focused on developing, manufacturing, and commercializing branded and generic prescription drugs, with a growing rare disease franchise (Cortrophin Gel). It sells to wholesalers, specialty pharmacies, and hospital systems. Revenue derives from both branded drugs with pricing power and a generic portfolio providing volume.
Industry: Specialty / Rare Disease Pharmaceuticals · vs: Mallinckrodt, Endo International, Hikma Pharmaceuticals

"Wall Street Blindspot: $1.8B Mcap sits right at the institutional size threshold where most mid-cap desks won't initiate and small-cap desks are exiting — classic orphaned transition zone with ~2 sell-side analysts; 44% revenue growth (accelerating from 26%), 69% gross margin, CR=2.7, and PEG=0.5 in a Healthcare sector averaging PEG=2.3 represents extreme mispricing for a profitable, cash-generative rare disease platform."

ANI is executing a textbook transition from generic commodity pharma to a branded rare disease compounder but is orphaned by sell-side because it just exited the small-cap box without yet attracting mid-cap coverage.

Price $82 P/E 20.4 PEG 0.5 MktCap $1.8B
MDXG Small Cap MiMedx Group, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH Fast Grower Asset Play ▼
83% gross margin with PE=10.5 and PEG=0.8 post-restructuring
🏢 About
MiMedx develops and markets regenerative biologics products derived from human placental tissue for wound care, surgical, and sports medicine applications. Its products are sold primarily to hospitals, wound care clinics, and outpatient surgical centers across the US. Revenue is product-based with a direct sales force model.
Industry: Regenerative Medicine / Wound Care Biologics · vs: Integra LifeSciences, Organogenesis, Smith+Nephew

"Wall Street Blindspot: $0.5B Mcap post-restructuring company (emerged from accounting scandal and restatement) still avoided by mandated institutional investors with clean-sheet compliance requirements — estimated 1 sell-side analyst; 83% gross margin (highest in candidate set), 15% operating margin, PE=10.5 on 20% growth = PEG=0.8, and CR=4.3 signal a fully rehabilitated, high-quality compounder still wearing a scarlet letter."

Post-restatement stigma keeps this 83%-gross-margin wound care compounder at a deep discount years after it cleaned up its books and reaccelerated growth.

Price $3 P/E 10.5 PEG 0.8 MktCap $0.5B
VITL Small Cap Vital Farms, Inc. · Consumer Defensive MEDIUM Fast Grower ▼
PE=8.5 on 25% consistent revenue growth = PEG=0.4 for a profitable branded food company
🏢 About
Vital Farms produces and sells pasture-raised eggs and butter under the Vital Farms brand through US grocery, club, and natural food retail channels. It sources from a network of small family farms under long-term supply agreements and commands significant retail shelf premium positioning. Revenue is product-based with strong brand loyalty and expanding SKU penetration.
Industry: Premium Branded Agriculture / Pasture-Raised Food · vs: Pete and Gerry's Organics, Kirkland (Costco private label), Cal-Maine Foods

"Wall Street Blindspot: $0.6B Mcap consumer staples company misclassified under Consumer Defensive where most analysts cover multi-billion dollar CPG giants — estimated 1-2 sell-side analysts; 25% revenue growth (consistent 100% RevConsist), PE=8.5, PEG=0.4, and 38% gross margin for a branded food company are metrics that belong to a $2B+ stock; institutional size economics prevent ownership at this cap."

The market assigns a commodity egg-producer multiple to a premium brand compounder with software-company revenue growth rates, purely because it's too small for institutional mandates.

Price $13 P/E 8.5 PEG 0.4 MktCap $0.6B
COLL Small Cap Collegium Pharmaceutical · Healthcare MEDIUM Fast Grower ▼
23% operating margin with 24% revenue growth at PEG=0.7 in a sin-stock-discounted sector
🏢 About
Collegium Pharmaceutical acquires and commercializes specialty branded pain management and CNS drugs, including Belbuca (buprenorphine) and Xtampza ER (oxycodone), both abuse-deterrent formulations. It sells through a direct specialty sales force to pain management physicians, anesthesiologists, and primary care physicians. Revenue is entirely branded prescription drug sales with strong recurring refill dynamics.
Industry: Specialty Pain Management / CNS Pharmaceuticals · vs: Teva Pharmaceutical, Purdue Pharma (legacy), Assertio Holdings

"Wall Street Blindspot: $1.1B Mcap specialty pharma in the politically toxic opioid-adjacent space creates a structural sin-stock discount — institutional ESG mandates and reputational risk force sell-side to avoid initiating (estimated 2 analysts); despite this, 59% gross margin, 23% operating margin, 24% revenue growth, and PEG=0.7 vs. Healthcare sector's 2.3 average indicates severe structural undervaluation driven by sector aversion rather than business quality."

ESG mandates and opioid stigma impose a structural discount on a profitable, growing specialty pharma with best-in-class margins that has zero exposure to legacy liability.

Price $34 P/E 16.6 PEG 0.7 MktCap $1.1B
🌍 Macro Dashboard — FRED data as of 2026-04-17

AI MARKET SYNOPSIS

The market is making a systematic error: it is applying recession-era multiples to a subset of high-ROIC compounders while simultaneously ignoring the earnings-inflection stories hidden inside 'boring' or 'stigmatized' categories. With the yield curve now positively sloped at +55bps, VIX at a calm 17.5, and unemployment still healthy at 4.3%, the macro does not justify the deep discounts visible in names like QLYS, HRB, PTCT, and PLMR. The real opportunity lies in businesses where consensus is anchored to a stale narrative — AI-disruption fears for HRB and ADBE, patent-cliff fears for PTCT, legacy-vendor fears for QLYS — while actual FCF generation and ROIC have already proved the thesis wrong.

🌍 GEOPOLITICAL & MACRO CONTEXT

The 10Y Treasury at 4.32% sets a meaningful hurdle — only businesses with FCF yield above 4% or a credible near-term earnings step-change earn a premium over risk-free. The yield curve at +55bps (normal) and VIX at 17.5 (calm) argue against a near-term recession or credit event, giving quality compounders time to let their fundamentals re-rate. CPI at 3.29% keeps the Fed from pivoting aggressively (Fed Funds at 3.64% — nearly neutral), meaning rate-sensitive multiple expansion is not the thesis; instead, earnings-driven re-rating is the only reliable path to returns, which further favors ROIC-compounders over speculative growth.

🔄 Cyclicals

Filter: Cyclical sectors (Industrials, Energy, Materials, Consumer Cyclical) at earnings trough. Lynch: P/E is INVERTED for cyclicals — HIGH or missing P/E = depressed earnings = BUY. LOW P/E = peak earnings = SELL. Best entry: decline decelerating + FCF positive + net debt < 3× EBITDA.
